title: viola alba subsp. scotophylla
intro: Viola alba subsp. scotophylla, also known as the Scottish dog violet, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the violet family (Violaceae). It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant is known for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing pain, improving wound healing, and protecting against cell damage. Viola alba subsp. scotophylla can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in folk medicine to treat conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems.
